texto: My mother moved abroad when I was 11, my dad wasn't around from the time that I was a baby, so I was not the product of a family, but a product of observation - of watching what went on around me, of watching who I liked, what I didn't like, what I thought was good behavior and what I thought was bad behavior and tailoring myself accordingly.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mark Strong: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “My mother moved abroad when I was 11, my dad wasn't around from the time that I was a baby, so I …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, time, good pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e good.
